topics.title: Present Perfect
topics.explanation: We use "have/has + past participle" for experiences or actions with a result now, without saying exactly when.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "have/has + التصريف الثالث" للخبرات أو الأفعال التي لها نتيجة الآن.

examples.en: I have visited Paris three times.
She has never eaten sushi.
We have already finished our project.
Have you ever seen a real lion?
examples.ar: هل رأيت أسدًا حقيقيًا من قبل؟

topics.title: First Conditional
topics.explanation: We use "If + present simple, ... will + verb" to talk about real possibilities in the future.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "If + مضارع بسيط، ... will + فعل" للحديث عن احتمالات حقيقية في المستقبل.

examples.en: If it rains, I will stay home.
If you study hard, you will pass the exam.
examples.ar: إذا ذاكرت بجد، ستنجح في الامتحان.

examples.en: She will be happy if you call her.
If we hurry, we won't miss the bus.

topics.title: Present Perfect vs Past Simple
topics.explanation: Use past simple for a finished action at a specific time. Use present perfect when the exact time isn't important, or the action connects to now.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم الماضي البسيط لفعل انتهى في وقت محدد. نستخدم المضارع التام عندما لا يكون الوقت مهمًا.

examples.en: I visited London last year.
I have visited London twice.
She finished her homework at six.
She has already finished her homework.

topics.title: Present Perfect with For and Since
topics.titleAr: for و since مع المضارع التام
topics.explanation: Use "for" with a period of time (for three years), and "since" with a specific starting point (since 2020).
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "for" مع مدة زمنية، و"since" مع نقطة بداية محددة.

examples.en: I have lived here for five years.
She has known him since childhood.
We have studied English for two years.
He has worked here since 2019.

topics.title: Second Conditional
topics.explanation: We use "If + past simple, ... would + verb" to talk about imaginary or unlikely situations.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "If + ماضي بسيط، ... would + فعل" للحديث عن مواقف تخيلية أو غير محتملة.

examples.en: If I had a million dollars, I would travel the world.
examples.ar: لو كان لدي مليون دولار، لسافرت حول العالم.

examples.en: If she studied more, she would get better grades.
examples.ar: لو ذاكرت أكثر، لحصلت على درجات أفضل.

examples.en: What would you do if you won the lottery?
examples.ar: ماذا كنت ستفعل لو ربحت اليانصيب؟

examples.en: I would help you if I could.

topics.title: Used to
topics.explanation: We use "used to" for past habits or states that are not true anymore.
topics.explanationAr: نستخدم "used to" للعادات أو الحالات الماضية التي لم تعد صحيحة الآن.

examples.en: I used to live in Alexandria.
She used to play the piano every day.
examples.ar: كانت تعزف على البيانو كل يوم.

examples.en: We didn't use to have a car.
Did you use to watch this show as a kid?
examples.ar: هل كنت تشاهد هذا البرنامج عندما كنت طفلاً؟

topics.title: Question Tags
topics.explanation: A short question added to the end of a sentence to confirm information, like "isn't it?" or "don't you?".
topics.explanationAr: سؤال قصير يُضاف إلى نهاية الجملة للتأكد من معلومة.

examples.en: This is your book, isn't it?
You like pizza, don't you?
She isn't coming, is she?
They can swim, can't they?

topics.title: Too / Enough
topics.explanation: "Too" means more than necessary (a problem). "Enough" means the right amount, and comes after the adjective.
topics.explanationAr: "too" تعني أكثر من اللازم. "enough" تعني الكمية المناسبة وتأتي بعد الصفة.

examples.en: This coffee is too hot to drink.
I'm not tall enough to reach the shelf.
examples.ar: لست طويلاً بما يكفي للوصول إلى الرف.

examples.en: This box is too heavy for me to carry.
Is this room big enough for the party?
examples.ar: هل هذه الغرفة كبيرة بما يكفي للحفلة؟
